project_structure: Monorepo with three packages: ccc-state-controller (Cocos Creator extension with panel UI and installer), ccc-state-controller-core (core TypeScript state management library), ccc-smart-component-manager (component management extension). Test infrastructure in tests/ follows Jest patterns for Cocos Creator with mocks directory. UI panels use Cocos Creator 2.x Editor.Panel.extend() pattern with inline style/template strings.

relevant_files.rationale: Comprehensive testing patterns documentation for Cocos Creator Jest tests, defining UI component vs pure logic strategies, mock setup order, describe organization (Lifecycle, UI Update, Event Handlers, Edge Cases), and quality assurance checklists

key_code.description: Defines beforeEach pattern: create scene, create node with active=false, addComponent instantiation, bind UI nodes - critical for new test implementation
Four-block structure: Lifecycle Methods, UI Update Methods, Event Handlers, Edge Cases - standard for organizing test files
Mutation testing checklist and anti-patterns (avoid testing mock itself, avoid repeating logic, avoid over-mocking)

relevant_files.topic_relation: Patterns exploration identifies this as the authoritative reference for unit test patterns - defines test structure, mock strategies, and quality criteria

relevant_files.rationale: Current ccc-state-controller panel implementation using Editor.Panel.extend() pattern with inline CSS/template, IPC communication to main process, state management for installation workflow

key_code.description: Cocos Creator 2.x panel pattern with style, template, $ (element bindings), ready(), _bindEvents(), messages (IPC handlers)
Internal state pattern: isInstalled, isInstalling, currentVersion, availableVersion, targetPath, corePath
Editor.Ipc.sendToMain() for main process calls with callback pattern - standard for Cocos extension panels

relevant_files.topic_relation: Patterns exploration targets this file as the UI refactoring candidate - current pattern uses inline styles/templates, should be refactored using ui-ux-pro-max

relevant_files.rationale: Integration test patterns for installer module covering fresh install, version update, validation, rollback scenarios with test context creation and cleanup

key_code.description: Test context factory returning dir, coreDir, targetDir, cleanup function - reusable pattern for isolation
Custom test runner tracking results, catching errors, logging pass/fail - follows GIVEN-WHEN-THEN
Test suites organized by scenario: Fresh Installation, Version Update, Validation, Rollback, Edge Cases

relevant_files.topic_relation: Patterns exploration identifies this as the integration test pattern reference for installer functionality - demonstrates scenario-based testing approach

relevant_files.rationale: TypeScript unit test patterns for core library with Jest mocks for Cocos Creator environment (cc, Editor globals), comprehensive coverage of state management, BFS cache, state operations

key_code.description: Global mock pattern for cc and Editor namespaces with jest.fn() stubs - required for Cocos Creator testing
Test coverage annotation pattern linking tests to implementation requirements
Three-level nesting: Feature (IMPL-006.2), Sub-feature (State Switching), Specific test cases (Boundary Handling)

relevant_files.topic_relation: Patterns exploration identifies core library test patterns - TypeScript mock setup, nested describe blocks for feature coverage

relevant_files.rationale: UI/UX design intelligence skill with 67 styles, 96 palettes, workflow for design system generation, stack-specific guidelines - key reference for UI refactoring

key_code.description: Design system generation command: python3 search.py '<query>' --design-system -p 'Project Name' --persist
Quality checklist: no emoji icons, cursor-pointer, light/dark contrast, responsive at 375/768/1024/1440px, accessibility
Anti-patterns: no emoji icons, stable hover states, correct brand logos, cursor-pointer, glass card visibility

relevant_files.rationale: Reference implementation for Cocos Creator panel with modern patterns: i18n integration, data manager, logger, SVG icons, separated logic from build

CommonJS imports for i18n-helper, data-manager, logger - modular architecture for panel logic

relevant_files.rationale: Jest configuration for Cocos Creator test environment, defines testMatch patterns, module paths, coverage settings

patterns:
## Test Patterns

### UI Component Test Pattern (from TESTING_GUIDE.md)
1. Mock before import: `jest.mock('path', () => ({ ... }))`
2. Create scene in beforeEach: `new cc.Scene()`, `cc.director.runSceneImmediate(scene)`
3. Node creation with inactive: `node.active = false` prevents premature onLoad
4. Component instantiation: `node.addComponent(MyComponent)`
5. UI binding: Manually set `instance['label'] = labelNode.addComponent(cc.Label)`
6. Four describe blocks: Lifecycle, UI Update, Event Handlers, Edge Cases

### Pure Logic Test Pattern
1. Mock external dependencies (App, API, Store)
2. Reset singleton: `(Class as any)._instance = null`
3. Mock platform APIs: `(globalThis as any).platform = { ... }`
4. Organize by feature modules

### Integration Test Pattern (from installer.test.js)
1. Test context factory: `createTestContext(scenarioName)` returns cleanup function
2. Scenario-based suites: Fresh Install, Update, Validation, Rollback, Edge Cases
3. Custom test runner with result tracking

## UI Patterns

### Cocos Creator Panel Pattern (Editor.Panel.extend)
1. `style:` - CSS string (Cocos dark theme: #333 background, #ccc text)
2. `template:` - HTML string with ui-* elements
3. `$:` - Element selector bindings
4. `ready()` - Initialization, state setup, event binding
5. `messages:` - IPC message handlers from main process

### Modern Panel Improvements (from smart-component-manager)
1. Separate logic.js from build.js
2. i18n integration via `const { t } = require('../i18n-helper')`
3. Inline SVG icons with currentColor for theming
4. Data manager and logger modules

### UI Refactoring Pattern (ui-ux-pro-max)
1. Generate design system: `python3 search.py '<query>' --design-system -p 'Project Name'`
2. Apply stack guidelines: `--stack html-tailwind` for Tailwind CSS
3. Follow pre-delivery checklist: no emoji icons, cursor-pointer, contrast 4.5:1+, responsive breakpoints
dependencies:
## Test Dependencies
- Jest (test framework)
- @types/jest (TypeScript types)
- ts-jest (TypeScript Jest transformer)
- Jest mocks for Cocos Creator: cc, Editor globals

## Panel Dependencies
- Cocos Creator 2.x Editor API
- Editor.Panel.extend() pattern
- Editor.Ipc for main process communication
- ui-input, ui-button elements (Cocos UI components)

## Core Library Dependencies
- Cocos Creator cc namespace
- cc.Component base class
- cc._decorator (ccclass, property, menu)

## UI Refactoring Dependencies
- ui-ux-pro-max skill (design system generator)
- Python 3 (for search.py script)
- SVG icons (no emoji requirement)
integration_points:
## Unit Test Integration
- tests/TESTING_GUIDE.md:L24-L87 defines UI component test pattern
- tests/jest.config.js defines testMatch and coverage
- tests/mocks/ contains shared mock utilities
- packages/ccc-state-controller/tests/ needs expansion following core test patterns

## UI Panel Integration
- packages/ccc-state-controller/panel/index.js:L383-L399 ready() initialization
- packages/ccc-state-controller/panel/index.js:L401-L427 _bindEvents() event handlers
- packages/ccc-state-controller/panel/index.js:L759-L778 messages IPC handlers
- packages/ccc-state-controller/main.js handles IPC from panel

## UI-UX Pro Max Integration
- .claude/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py --design-system command
- Generate design system then apply to panel styles
- Follow Pre-Delivery Checklist (SKILL.md:L344-L377)

## Test-Code Integration
- packages/ccc-state-controller-core/tests/*.test.ts reference patterns
- packages/ccc-state-controller/tests/installer.test.js integration patterns
- New tests should follow TESTING_GUIDE.md structure
constraints:
## Test Constraints
1. Mock before import - Jest hoisting requires jest.mock() before imports
2. Node active=false required - Prevents premature onLoad triggering
3. cc.Component inheritance required for test doubles
4. Minimum 4 describe blocks for UI components (Lifecycle, UI Update, Events, Edge Cases)
5. No testing mock itself - test real logic with mocked dependencies

## UI Constraints
1. Cocos Creator 2.x Editor.Panel.extend() pattern required
2. Inline CSS strings (no external stylesheets)
3. ui-input, ui-button elements (Cocos custom elements)
4. IPC communication via Editor.Ipc.sendToMain()
5. Dark theme compatibility (#333 background, #ccc text minimum contrast)

## UI Refactoring Constraints
1. No emoji icons - use SVG only
2. cursor-pointer on all interactive elements
3. 4.5:1 contrast ratio minimum
4. Responsive at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px
5. prefers-reduced-motion support
6. Both light and dark mode visibility (for future Cocos versions)

## Project Constraints
1. TypeScript for core library, JavaScript for extension panels
2. CommonJS modules (require/module.exports)
3. Jest for all testing
4. Maintain backward compatibility with existing installations

clarification_needs.question: What is the scope of unit test coverage expansion for ccc-state-controller?
clarification_needs.context: The ccc-state-controller package currently has installer.test.js but may need additional unit tests for panel UI logic and installer modules. TESTING_GUIDE.md provides patterns but scope needs definition.
options: Full coverage: Test all installer modules (file-copier, validator, version-manager) + panel logic
Critical path only: Test installer/index.js main functions + panel IPC handlers
Incremental: Add tests for untested modules as bugs are found
Match core coverage: Mirror test coverage of ccc-state-controller-core

clarification_needs.question: What UI style direction should the panel refactoring follow?
clarification_needs.context: ui-ux-pro-max supports 67 styles (glassmorphism, minimalism, brutalism, etc.). The current panel uses dark theme (#333, #ccc) with Cocos Creator conventions. Need to choose a style direction.
options: Maintain current dark theme with modern polish (improve contrast, add transitions, better spacing)
Glassmorphism dark mode (frosted glass effect, blur, subtle borders)
Minimalism dark mode (clean lines, reduced visual noise, focused content)
Generate design system via ui-ux-pro-max and follow its recommendation

clarification_needs.question: Should panel logic be separated into modules like smart-component-manager?
clarification_needs.context: ccc-smart-component-manager/panel/logic.js demonstrates separation of concerns: i18n-helper, data-manager, logger imports. Current ccc-state-controller/panel/index.js is monolithic (780 lines).
options: Keep monolithic - simpler for single-purpose installer panel
Extract utilities (logger, version-compare, ipc-helpers) into separate modules
Full refactor: Separate logic.js, styles.js, templates.js with build step
Only extract testable logic into separate modules
